title: Heavy mesh smoke test at production subdivision levels
description: Add a JUnit test tagged @Tag("heavy") that runs tool_quilt.dsl at the DSL's default subdivision level (6) with -Xmx4g. Asserts: completes within 10s, no OOM, vertex count in expected range for subdivision level, vertex positions show displacement. Excluded from the fast unit/mesh/*Test suite via surefire tag filtering, but agents must run it before completing mesh/DSL tickets. This is the missing verification step that would have caught the OOM before a user hit it.
definition-of-done: 1. A test class (e.g., HeavyMeshSmokeTest) tagged @Tag("heavy") exists. 2. It runs tool_quilt.dsl at subdivision level 6 (the DSL default, no override). 3. It asserts: mesh is non-null, vertex count > 100k, vertex positions show displacement (radial range > 0.001), completes within 10s. 4. The default surefire configuration excludes the 'heavy' tag. 5. A Maven command to run only heavy tests is documented: mvn test -Dgroups=heavy -Xmx4g.
testing-plan: 1. Run mvn test -Dtest='unit/mesh/*Test' and verify the heavy test is NOT included. 2. Run mvn test -Dgroups=heavy and verify the heavy test runs and passes. 3. Verify the test completes in <10s on a developer machine.
todos: Configure surefire to exclude 'heavy' tag by default
Add test that runs tool_quilt.dsl at default params (subdivisions=6)
Assert vertex count, displacement, and runtime budget
Document the mvn command for running heavy tests
unknowns: Whether surefire supports tag exclusion via pom.xml or needs a profile
Whether 10s is a realistic budget at subdivision 6 with the current HalfEdgeMesh implementation
